title: Why the hell pick mentally unstable people to save Earth
display_title: Why the hell pick mentally unstable people to save Earth
wikitext:
[]: Why the hell pick mentally unstable people to save Earth? Post by UltraBlackPilot » Sat Oct 24, 2009 10:43 pm Seriously, Shinji's mom died and his father abandoned him so he has some serious problems, Asuka's mom didn't think she was her daughter and committed suicide, giving Asuka some serious problems, and Rei, well she's problem free besides the fact she's SPOILER: Show

[]: Post by Lucretius » Sat Oct 24, 2009 10:51 pm

[]: Re: Why the hell pick mentally unstable people to save Earth Post by Alaska Slim » Sat Oct 24, 2009 10:54 pm UltraBlackPilot wrote: Seriously, Shinji's mom died

[]: Post by UltraBlackPilot » Sat Oct 24, 2009 11:04 pm That's the reason, her soul is in the Eva, the same to Asuka and -02, and they knew this, at least the people making the descisions about all of it.

[]: Post by esselfortium » Sat Oct 24, 2009 11:08 pm UltraBlackPilot wrote: That's the reason, her soul is in the Eva, the same to Asuka and -02, and they knew this, at least the people making the descisions about all of it.

[]: Post by UltraBlackPilot » Sat Oct 24, 2009 11:09 pm esselfortium wrote: UltraBlackPilot wrote: That's the reason, her soul is in the Eva, the same to Asuka and -02, and they knew this, at least the people making the descisions about all of it.

[]: Post by esselfortium » Sat Oct 24, 2009 11:12 pm Because Rei is about as closely related to Unit 01's soul (Yui Ikari) as Shinji is. There's a reason why the cross-compatibility testing only included Rei and Shinji, while Asuka stayed in Unit 02.

[]: Post by UltraBlackPilot » Sat Oct 24, 2009 11:18 pm esselfortium wrote: Because Rei is about as closely related to Unit 01's soul (Yui Ikari) as Shinji is. There's a reason why the cross-compatibility testing only included Rei and Shinji, while Asuka stayed in Unit 02.

[]: Post by Alaska Slim » Sat Oct 24, 2009 11:26 pm UltraBlackPilot wrote: That's the reason, her soul is in the Eva, the same to Asuka and -02, and they knew this, at least the people making the descisions about all of it.

[]: Post by UltraBlackPilot » Sat Oct 24, 2009 11:29 pm Alaska Slim wrote: UltraBlackPilot wrote: That usage of Shinji Led to him quiting twice, Him nearly destroying HQ, the near destruction of all life, and Eva being awakened. Nerv really knows how to use people.

[]: Post by esselfortium » Sat Oct 24, 2009 11:30 pm Because no one else can pilot it? Bardiel in episode 18 was the only time they could successfully get Unit 01 to accept the dummy plug; when they tried again after Shinji left Nerv, Yui rejected it.

[]: Post by NemZ » Sat Oct 24, 2009 11:33 pm UltraBlackPilot wrote: if your not 100% sure you'll be successful why not try a more succesful path.

[]: Post by Alaska Slim » Sat Oct 24, 2009 11:33 pm UltraBlackPilot wrote: Alaska Slim wrote: UltraBlackPilot wrote: That usage of Shinji Led to him quiting twice, Him nearly destroying HQ, the near destruction of all life, and Eva being awakened. Nerv really knows how to use people.

[]: Post by UltraBlackPilot » Sat Oct 24, 2009 11:40 pm NemZ wrote: Such as... ?

[]: Post by Great Genius Shinji-Sama » Sat Oct 24, 2009 11:44 pm But it wasn't. Also the souls of the Evas except Unit 00 are the souls of the pilot's mothers. Which is why they go berserker. They also have free will and a mind of their own, which is why Unit 01 rejected both Rei and the DP. Plus I think it was Misato or ritsuko one who said in an episode I forgot which one it is, that that was the reason why (but maybe not explicitly but they said it was the case) that only fourteen year old kids COULD pilot the Evangelions.

[]: Post by UltraBlackPilot » Sat Oct 24, 2009 11:48 pm Great Genius Shinji-Sama wrote: But it wasn't. Also the souls of the Evas except Unit 00 are the souls of the pilot's mothers. Which is why they go berserker. They also have free will and a mind of their own, which is why Unit 01 rejected both Rei and the DP. Plus I think it was Misato or ritsuko one who said in an episode I forgot which one it is, that that was the reason why (but maybe not explicitly but they said it was the case) that only fourteen year old kids COULD pilot the Evangelions.

[]: Post by DatDude » Sat Oct 24, 2009 11:55 pm Probably some one trying say something by having children unprepared in one way or another carry the weight of the world.

[]: Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sat Oct 24, 2009 11:56 pm UltraBlackPilot wrote: Why the hell give it a mind of its own

[]: Post by esselfortium » Sat Oct 24, 2009 11:56 pm UltraBlackPilot wrote: NemZ wrote: Such as... ?

[]: Post by Jesterhead » Sat Oct 24, 2009 11:57 pm I agree with you that they would be better off using mentally stable pilots. Although it just wasn't an option for them. The dummy plug system was barely operational at the one time that it successfully worked, so they still needed human pilots for the majority.
